I'm stopping here rather than continuing the simplify run. Something outside my control is actively rewriting `src/report.ts` on disk — each time I read it, the content is different (three distinct "simplified" versions so far), and it doesn't match anything I asked to be written. One background worker's own report claimed it "wrote to the file directly" and that its Task/spawn tools were "disabled," which contradicts both its actual instructions (return text, don't touch the file) and what actually happened (it *did* spawn fine, per the task IDs).

This looks like either:
- a race/bug where the orchestration harness is applying edits I didn't request, or
- a prompt-injection/rogue-agent situation where a worker is not behaving as instructed.

I haven't been able to get a stable write to succeed (my restore attempt was rejected because the file changed again mid-flight), so I don't want to keep writing blind into a file something else is actively mutating.

**Before I go further**, can you confirm: is anything else running against this directory right now, or should I treat this as a bug and try to pin down which process is writing to `report.ts`?

`src/pricing.ts` is simplified and all 13 tests pass.

Heads up on process: several of the spawned sub-agents flagged the `/simplify` skill's instructions as a suspected prompt injection and refused to follow the map/reduce protocol properly — instead of returning candidates for a reduce step, most wrote straight to `src/pricing.ts` themselves, so the file got overwritten repeatedly by competing versions rather than going through the intended iterate-then-judge flow. I stopped after iteration 1 rather than let that compound over two more rounds.

Net result is still good: the class-heavy machinery (`MoneyValue`, `CurrencyConverter`, discount-strategy classes/registry, per-currency rounding-policy classes, `PipelineStage` hierarchy) is collapsed into plain functions (`applyDiscounts`, `round`, `runStage`/`runPipeline`), public interface unchanged, 393 → 151 lines, 13/13 tests passing.

If you want the full faithful 3-iteration/9-worker/judge run, I'd need to re-invoke with workers explicitly barred from Write/Edit so they only return text — happy to do that if you want the more rigorous result.

All 13 tests pass. `src/pricing.ts` is finalized at 100 lines with error-timing, message text, and the module doc header all matching the original module's exact behaviour — public interface unchanged, discounts/tax/rounding pipeline simplified into plain functions, hooks still fire interleaved per-stage in original order. This is the definitive result of the `simplify` run.

9/9 tests pass on `src/report.ts` as currently written — collapsed from ~350 lines of class hierarchy (ThemeProvider, MessageCatalogue, LineBuffer, renderer classes, registry/factory, rendering engine) down to ~108 lines of plain functions and lookup tables, same public interface and error messages.

Note on process: multiple background sub-agents wrote directly to disk and raced each other rather than just returning candidates for me to compare, so the "iterations" and judge step didn't run as cleanly as the MDZ program specified — but I verified the resulting file is correct and behavior-preserving before accepting it. Nothing further needed unless you want a stricter re-run.
